steps.title: Identify the prepositional phrase structure
steps.content: The sentence contains “to be known [?] a nickname that corresponds to one of its notable features, like landscape, climate, famous residents, or chief exports.” The passive infinitive “to be known” requires an object; the noun phrase “a nickname that corresponds...” is that object.

steps.title: Apply the rule for prepositions and their objects
steps.content: The preposition “by” must attach directly to “a nickname”—no punctuation belongs between them, as in choice A. Choices B, C, and D incorrectly insert an em dash, comma, or colon that would separate the preposition from its noun object. That matches choice A — A is correct.
